title: About Molluscum Contagiosum
license_notes: Use as public patient-education source support. Avoid verbatim copying into primitives.
supports: Molluscum contagiosum is a poxvirus infection that causes skin lesions that can appear alone or in groups.
The infection is usually mild and goes away without treatment.
Molluscum can spread by touching lesions or contaminated objects.
Avoid scratching or shaving over lesions to reduce spread.
